title: NSX Edge Gateway Health
spl:
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status" earliest=-4h
| where overall_status!="UP" OR bgp_status!="Established"
| stats latest(overall_status) as st, latest(bgp_status) as bgp by edge_node, lr_name
| table edge_node, lr_name, st, bgp
description: Service status for Tier-0/Tier-1 SR components (BGP, NAT, LB service) on Edge nodes — complements CPU metrics (UC-18.2.4).
value: Service status for Tier-0/Tier-1 SR components (BGP, NAT, LB service) on Edge nodes — complements CPU metrics (UC-18.2.4).
grandmaExplanation: Service status for Tier-0/Tier-1 SR components (, LB service) on Edge nodes — complements CPU metrics (UC-18.2.4) — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
implementation: Map status fields from NSX Manager API. Alert on any non-UP gateway service. Correlate with northbound ISP events.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `vmware_nsx_addon`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `vmware:nsx:edge_status`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map status fields from NSX Manager API. Alert on any non-UP gateway service. Correlate with northbound ISP events.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status" earliest=-4h
| where overall_status!="UP" OR bgp_status!="Established"
| stats latest(overall_status) as st, latest(bgp_status) as bgp by edge_node, lr_name
| table edge_node, lr_name, st, bgp
```

Understanding this SPL

**NSX Edge Gateway Health** — Service status for Tier-0/Tier-1 SR components (BGP, NAT, LB service) on Edge nodes — complements CPU metrics (UC-18.2.4).

Documented **Data sources**: `vmware:nsx:edge_status`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `vmware_nsx_addon`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vmware; **sourcetype**: vmware:nsx:edge_status. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vmware, sourcetype="vmware:nsx:edge_status", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where overall_status!="UP" OR bgp_status!="Established"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by edge_node, lr_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Pipeline stage (see **NSX Edge Gateway Health**): table edge_node, lr_name, st, bgp


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (edge × LR), Table (down services), Timeline.
visualization: Status grid (edge × LR), Table (down services), Timeline.
